dashboard.report-content.emi.for-each-de: For each detected damage type, the system calculates a partial score reflecting the severity of the defect. These partial scores are then aggregated via a standardized weighting model into a single overall grade — the condition class of the respective segment.
dashboard.report-content.emi.network-sur:
Network survey — camera vehicle covers all segments; a georeferenced photo is taken every 13 ft.
Automated image analysis — algorithms detect the presence of 15 defined damage classes.
Segment scoring — detected damage is weighted and aggregated into a single overall condition score per segment.
Overview & export — all results are available interactively in the vialytics web system: map view, segment lists, charts, and export.
dashboard.report-content.emi.the-road-ne: The road network is systematically surveyed using a camera-equipped vehicle. The camera triggers automatically every four meters and records each image together with its GPS coordinates. After the survey, an AI-powered system analyzes all imagery fully automatically — without manual assessments influencing the result.
dashboard.report-content.emi.the-vialyti: The vialytics analysis delivers detailed data for every road segment. At the core of the assessment is the automatic detection of 15 standardized damage types, covering the full spectrum of asphalt surface defects.
dashboard.report-content.emi.vialytics-u:
vialytics uses the node-edge principle: each road segment is defined as an “edge” between two “nodes” — typically intersections or junctions. This network model forms the spatial reference frame to which all photos and assessments are uniquely assigned.

For each segment, the database stores essential data: a unique identifier, road category, surface type, length and width, and street name. Additional attributes — such as sidewalk presence or lane markings — can be added optionally.
dashboard.report-content.emi.what-is-via: In brief: vialytics captures georeferenced photos of pavement surfaces during regular drive-throughs. Algorithms automatically analyze the imagery for damage and calculate a condition score for each road segment — based on the internationally recognized E-EMI-2012 methodology.

vialytics is a digital system for visually automated pavement condition assessment. It enables municipalities, cities, and counties to document the condition of their road network objectively, efficiently, and reproducibly — creating a reliable data foundation for strategic road maintenance and budget planning.

Unlike traditional field inspections by technical staff, vialytics relies on camera-equipped vehicle surveys and AI-based image analysis. The result: broader network coverage in less time, significantly reduced personnel effort, and a standardized assessment outcome that is always reproducible, independent of the individual assessor.
dashboard.report-content.evaluation-meth:
The evaluation is based on the Recommendations for Maintenance Management of Urban Roads (E-EMI 2012). This standard was specifically developed for municipal road networks and methodologically follows the ZEB guideline, which is applied to federal highways.

The resulting condition score falls on a scale from 1 (very good) to 5 (very poor) and allows a direct comparison between segments — regardless of when the survey took place or who conducted it.

Action threshold: According to E-EMI guidelines, the score range from 3.0 to 3.5 marks the threshold at which substance-preserving measures should be initiated. The specific measure appropriate depends on the particular distress pattern of the segment in question.
E-EMI 2012 – Recommendations for Maintenance Management of Urban Roads
dashboard.report-content.paser.damages:
10–9 — Excellent: new or nearly new pavement, no visible distress
8–7 — Good: minor surface defects only, no structural issues
6–5 — Fair: moderate cracking or surface deterioration, preventive treatment recommended
4–3 — Poor: extensive cracking, patching, or rutting, rehabilitation needed
2–1 — Very poor / Failed: reconstruction recommended
dashboard.report-content.paser.for-each-: For each segment, the assigned PASER score directly indicates the recommended treatment urgency and informs prioritization within the vialytics maintenance planning workflow.
dashboard.report-content.paser.the-road-: The road network is systematically driven with a camera vehicle. Photos are taken every 13 feet, each associated with GPS coordinates. After the survey, an AI system rates all imagery automatically, assigning PASER scores based on observed surface condition — without requiring trained raters to physically inspect each segment.
dashboard.report-content.paser.the-vialy: The vialytics PASER assessment delivers a 1–10 condition score per road segment, where 10 is new or excellent pavement and 1 is failed. Ratings are based on visible surface distress indicators consistent with the PASER rating guide, developed by the University of Wisconsin–Madison Transportation Information Center.
dashboard.report-content.paser.vialytics:
vialytics uses the node-edge model: each road segment is defined as an edge between two nodes (intersections or endpoints). All imagery and PASER scores are uniquely assigned to their precise segment location.

For each segment, the database stores essential data: a unique identifier, road category, surface type, length and width, and street name. Additional attributes can be added optionally.
dashboard.report-content.paser.what-is-v: In brief: vialytics captures georeferenced photos of pavement surfaces during a standard drive-through. Algorithms automatically analyze imagery and assign a PASER rating (1–10) to each road segment — following the Wisconsin Transportation Information Center (LTAP) methodology widely adopted across the Midwest and beyond.

vialytics is a digital system for visually automated pavement condition assessment. It enables municipalities, counties, and local agencies to document the condition of their road network objectively, efficiently, and reproducibly — creating a reliable data foundation for maintenance planning and budget prioritization.

Unlike traditional drive-by or walk inspections, vialytics uses camera-equipped vehicles and AI-powered image analysis. The result: faster network-wide coverage, reduced field personnel cost, and a consistent, observer-independent PASER rating.
dashboard.report-content.pci.damages:
Alligator cracking
Block cracking
Longitudinal and transverse cracking
Patching and utility cut patching
Potholes
Raveling
Filled cracks
dashboard.report-content.pci.evaluation-:
The scoring approach is aligned with the Standard Practice for Roads and Parking Lots Pavement Condition Index Surveys (ASTM D6433) — the pavement condition standard most widely adopted by US municipalities, counties, and state DOTs. vialytics adapts this deduct-value framework for fully automated, image-based analysis of 100% of the network, rather than the statistical sampling used in manual ASTM field surveys — enabling consistent, network-wide scoring on the same 0–100 scale.
The resulting PCI score falls on a scale from 100 (excellent) to 0 (failed) and allows a direct comparison between segments — regardless of when the survey took place or who conducted it.

Action threshold: A PCI below 40 marks the threshold at which structural — rather than preventive — measures should be planned. The specific treatment depends on the distress pattern of the segment in question.
ASTM D6433 – Standard Practice for Pavement Condition Index Surveys
dashboard.report-content.pci.for-each-de: For each detected distress, the system applies a deduct-value curve based on density and severity. All deduct values are combined using the corrected deduct value (CDV) method to produce a final PCI score per segment — on a scale from 0 (failed) to 100 (excellent).
dashboard.report-content.pci.network-sur:
Network survey — camera vehicle covers all segments; a georeferenced photo is taken every 13 ft.
Automated distress detection — algorithms identify distress type and severity aligned with ASTM D6433 categories.
PCI calculation — distress density and severity are combined using the standard PCI deduct value methodology to produce a 0–100 score per segment.
Results & export — all PCI scores are available in the vialytics web system with map view, segment lists, condition distribution charts, and data export.
dashboard.report-content.pci.the-road-ne: The road network is surveyed systematically using a camera vehicle. Images are captured every 13 feet, each geo-tagged with GPS coordinates. After the survey, AI algorithms analyze all imagery automatically to identify ASTM-defined distress types and severities — without manual walkthroughs or subjective assessor judgment.
dashboard.report-content.pci.the-vialyti: The vialytics PCI assessment delivers a standardized condition score (0–100) for each road segment, plus the underlying distress data. The AI detects ASTM-defined distress types at three severity levels (Low / Medium / High).
dashboard.report-content.pci.vialytics-u:
vialytics structures the road network using a node-edge model: each pavement section is defined as an edge between two nodes (typically intersections). This provides the spatial reference frame for assigning all imagery and condition scores to precise locations.

For each segment, the database stores essential data: a unique identifier, road category, surface type, length and width, and street name. Additional attributes can be added optionally.
dashboard.report-content.pci.what-is-via: In brief: vialytics captures georeferenced photos of pavement surfaces during a standard drive-through. Algorithms automatically detect distress types and severities, then calculate a Pavement Condition Index (PCI) score for each segment — the ASTM-standardized 0–100 scale used by agencies across the US.

vialytics is a digital system for visually automated pavement condition assessment. It enables municipalities, cities, and counties to document the condition of their road network objectively, efficiently, and reproducibly — providing a reliable data foundation for strategic asset management and capital planning.
Unlike traditional windshield or walk-and-chalk inspections, vialytics uses camera-equipped vehicles and AI-powered image analysis. The result: network-wide coverage faster than manual methods, reduced personnel cost, and a consistent, assessor-independent rating that aligns with ASTM D6433 standards.

datasynchronization.nonetwork:
You are currently offline.

This means:
- No live notifications or real-time updates.
- You cannot change the title, priority, assignee or due date of a Work Order.
- Linked Asset, cost data, documents and changelog of a Work Order will not be displayed.
- Existing Assets, Track Controls and Winter Services will not be displayed.

However, you can still:
- Create new Work Orders, Assets, Track Controls, and Winter Services without restrictions.
- These will sync automatically once you're back online.

We recommend:
- Enabling offline availability for Work Orders and map in the app settings
- Using the ToDo app with a SIM card for the best experience

frenchtreatments.description.pata: Externalisé. Le point à temps automatique est une technique avec machine qui a pour objet la réparation des couches de roulement des chaussées en des points ciblés (sur mesure) où celles-ci ont subi des dégradations de surface
frenchtreatments.description.pontagefiss: Externalisé. Utiliser généralement pour faire du curatif et ponter les fissures er les joints la durée de vie dépendra de plusieurs facteurs comme le climat et le traffic
frenchtreatments.description.renouvellem: Externalisé. Enduit superficiel résistant comprenant de l’enrobé coulé à chaud et de l’enrobé coulé à froid. Important de prendre en compte la déformation avant de choisir cette technique.

Externalisé. L’enduit superficiel d'usure (parfois abrégé en ESU) est une couche de roulement réalisée en place et constituée de 2 couches de liant hydrocarboné et de granulats répandues successivement pour des dégradations de surfaces. Important de prendre en compte la déformation avant de choisir cette technique
Externalisé. Enduit superficiel comprenant de l’enrobé projeté et de l’enrobé coulé à froid. Important de prendre en compte la déformation avant de choisir cette technique.

newfeaturemodal.condition.content:
Creating a reliable data foundation is essential for informed decision-making and efficient road management. With the new vialytics Road Database, you have a centralized software solution designed for professional, efficient road oversight.

Effortlessly organize your roads within a fully customizable table, giving you the flexibility to add unique attributes, change road classifications, name unknown roads, set surface types, or exclude sections from evaluation.
newfeaturemodal.plannedsegments.content:
Customize your planning list according to your requirements. Add your own user-defined properties to the planning list. Organize the columns as you wish and edit your planning list directly in the table. New filters are also available for a better overview. 
Watch the video and familiarize yourself with the new functions!
newfeaturemodal.plannedsegments.header: New functions available for the planning list! 🚀
newfeaturemodal.trafficsigns.content: Now you can interact with your traffic signs. Verify or delete AI-detected traffic signs on your own. Moreover, you can create traffic signs out of the system, edit existing traffic signs, and attach documents to each traffic sign.

plannedsegments.budget.featurereleasemod:
Planning List becomes Budget Planner
Use it to plan your annual budget and always keep the total amount in view.

Calculate the Remaining Service Life
A complete planning list and the specification of Remaining Service Life for individual measures are essential for a condition forecast. If both apply, the sum of kilometers*years will indicate whether enough is being done to maintain the value of your road network.

Calculate the Remaining Service Life
A complete planning list and the specification of Remaining Service Life for individual measures are essential for a condition forecast. If both apply, the sum of miles*years will indicate whether enough is being done to maintain the value of your road network.

rdb.exlcude.modal.headline: Disable Grading for Road Classification
rdb.exlcude.modal.text:
You are about to disable grading for the selected road classification’s pavement condition and  marking condition (if applicable). This change will affect all segments using this classification, and grading data will no longer be available for them.

The affected segments will:

• Continue to count toward the total length.
• Appear in gray on the map for clarity.
• Retain point images for reference.

Please confirm to apply this change to all affected segments. These changes can be undone.

teaser.immodule.description:
Your advantages:

- Automatically detect traffic signs 🪧
- Automatically detect manholes and storm drains 🕳️
- Automatically detect damaged pavement markings 🛣️
- Receive Road Safety Alerts for detected hazards ⚠️
- Get an AI-generated overview of roadway assets 🛑


“We save about 60% of our time through reduced outdoor drives and less manual effort.”

— Sebastian Beel
Building Authority, Krailling, Germany
teaser.immodule.headline: Upgrade to Roadway Asset Copilot!
teaser.winterservicetaskmanagement.descr:
❄️ Winter Service:
• Document winter services automatically in a separate layer
• Document used operation mode (Salting/Plowing)
• Filter by date to protect yourself in insurance cases

☑️ Work Order Management:
• Assign Work Orders to people
• Set due dates
• Create recurring Work Orders

treatments.title: Title
usatreatments.description.basestabilizat: Improves load-bearing properties of aggregate base layer, conserving resources and reducing costs in low-quality aggregate areas.
usatreatments.description.capeseal: Combines chip seal with slurry/micro surfacing, sealing cracks, providing skid resistance, moisture protection, and a uniform appearance.
usatreatments.description.chipseal: Layered asphalt binder with aggregate, offers skid resistance, stops raveling, seals minor cracks, and prevents further deterioration.
usatreatments.description.coldcentralpla: Utilizes high-quality RAP for asphalt production, reducing landfill waste and project costs.
usatreatments.description.coldinplacerec: Process recycling top layers of asphalt, significantly reducing costs and resources for road repair.
usatreatments.description.coldplaningmic: Controlled removal of pavement surface to desired depth or slope for excavation, reconstruction, or maintenance.
usatreatments.description.crackseal: Cost-effective method to treat cracks greater than 1/8", preventing pavement failure like potholes.
usatreatments.description.fogseal: Light emulsified asphalt application on existing asphalt surfaces.
usatreatments.description.fulldepthrecla: Sustainable alternative to deep rehabilitation or replacement methods for asphalt pavements. It involves pulverizing the pavement and underlying materials to create a uniform, upgraded material, which can be further strengthened.
usatreatments.description.fulldepthremov: Rehabilitates asphalt pavement and underlying materials, enhancing structural capacity via mechanical, chemical, or bituminous stabilization.
usatreatments.description.hotinplacerecy: On-site technique for pavement preservation and corrective maintenance, improving recycled pavement characteristics.
usatreatments.description.microsurfacing: Economical, quick, treatment for good pavement, offering skid resistance, moisture control, oxidation protection, leveling.
usatreatments.description.scrubseal: Similar to chip seal but uses brooms to guide asphalt into cracks for thorough sealing.
usatreatments.description.slurryseal: Enhances skid resistance, restricts moisture, prevents oxidation, restores black appearance on good asphalt pavements.
usatreatments.description.soilstabilizat: Enhancing soil via additives like lime, fly ash, or cement reduces plasticity, bolsters strength, and improves workability for better load-bearing capacity in pavements and foundations.
usatreatments.description.ultrathinlifth: Asphalt treatment for smooth surfaces, addressing raveling, low-severity cracking, and oxidation.

workorders.workordermanagementplusteaser: Activate the Work Order Management Plus module to unlock more insights on your work orders.
Everything you need for professional Work Order management:
Reorder Work Order types, Categories, Templates, Time Tracking, Cost Centers, and more. Document seamlessly, save time, and stay organized — all in one simple yet flexible tool.

To align with our new module and better reflect commonly used terminology, we’re renaming a few key terms:
Tasks → Work Orders
Categories → Work Order Types
